column1: #FORMAT=WebAnno TSV 3.3
#T_SP=webanno.custom.CharacterMention|CharacterName
#T_RL=webanno.custom.SocialRelationship|Direction|InclusionRelationType|Modality|RelationType|BT_webanno.custom.CharacterMention
#Text=Repeat
#Text=Mornings have always felt disorienting to any individual, the slight warble that spills through one's mouth as the set of common fills your brain.

#Text=As your system finally is in full motion and your eyes flutter to the luminous sun rays blinding you from the windows, from the way the room inadvertently fills with the glow of blooming after thoughts, it tells you that you lived another night, and the world has set it for you to live until tomorrow.
#Text=If it were any other day, Tubbo might've just closed the curtains and fallen right back asleep.
#Text=He never really was one for mornings, the constant chatter of his mind screaming at him that work is more important made it impossible to enjoy mornings peacefully.
#Text=Especially with his internal clock that was programmed back during L'manberg times, waking up bright and early to possibly be a part of another war.
#Text=However, it wasn't like any other day, because Ranboo was finally staying home today.
#Text=It wasn't
#Text=that
#Text=much of a rare occurrence, contrary to popular belief; Ranboo was a busy person and often was out running errands or helping citizens with their tasks.
#Text=They're so kind to everyone, Tubbo kind of hates that in an endearing way.
#Text=A kind, loving, stupid bastard, they are.
#Text=Aside from that, they were mainly gone in the mornings, sheepishly coming back later in the afternoon with the same old
#Text=“I lost track of time and I saw this really cute cat on the sidewalk so—”
#Text=This time, though, Ranboo was holding Tubbo by the waist, hands relaxed into fists to avoid scratching Tubbo with his big cat claws.
#Text=His head was buried into Tubbo's neck, breathing in and out.
#Text=Both of their bodies were flushed into each other, poetically like puzzle pieces clicking so satisfyingly together.
#Text=Or maybe like slime, mixing two completely different types of slime but in the end, it's still slime, and it's still able to mold and adapt.
#Text=..That was sappy.
#Text=Tubbo can't find it in him to apologize, though.
#Text=Tubbo is awake, and he knows this, but he finds it so amazingly comforting to keep his eyes closed anyways.
#Text=It's like a reminder that he is
#Text=allowed
#Text=to spoil himself a little, pretending to sleep so he can pretend to wake up when Ranboo wakes up.
#Text=So they can pretend they were asleep too and give their low rumble of a
#Text=good morning
#Text=.
#Text=So he does just that.
#Text=He has his eyes shut but he's so tired anyways, it might've been him actually dozing off.
#Text=He feels Ranboo's ear twitch into his neck, and Tubbo shudders a little from it.
#Text=Tubbo doesn't move, though.
#Text=He wants Ranboo to do his silly routine of getting up and watching it happen.
#Text=Coincidentally enough, Ranboo shifts in the bed, somehow pulling Tubbo closer into him, and Tubbo can't help but stifle a lazy giggle from the action.
#Text=Ranboo has always been protective of his little family, but it's so apparent in the mornings.
#Text=Tubbo chalks it up to their enderman instincts flaring up in the early set of dawn, but sometimes, Tubbo can't help but think it's just
#Text=Ranboo.
#Text=Which makes him blush, just a tiny bit.
#Text=Ranboo groans, mumbling something under his breath but it all just sounds gibberish to Tubbo.
#Text=“G’mornin’ to you too, Boo.”
#Text=Tubbo whispers, and Ranboo grins in his neck.
#Text=“‘S not time yet..”
#Text=Ranboo whispers back, voice heavy and deep from the exhaustion.
#Text=Tubbo chuckles mutely, “time for what, exactly?”
#Text=Ranboo groans, sniffing harshly.
#Text=“To ge’up..” and Tubbo snorts at how tired they sound.
#Text=“Pretty sure ‘s like—” Tubbo pauses, “8 in the morning, bossman.
#Text=‘Reckon Michael will be up soon.
#Text=Gotta feed our baby his bottle.”
#Text=Tubbo starts listing things they had to do today, making Ranboo groan even louder.
#Text=The vibration of it tickles Tubbo's neck and he laughs a little louder than expected.
#Text=“But I wanna cuddle.
#Text=Just a li~ttle longer,” Ranboo draws out the ‘i’ in little, and Tubbo rolls his eyes.
#Text=He slaps Ranboo’s hands holding his waist, making him flinch in false startle.
#Text=“I'm going to start calling you mean names if you don't let me go, big man.”
#Text=Tubbo warns, but maybe that isn't very transparent by how he's smiling right now.
#Text=“You call me mean names all the time, I'm used to it.”
#Text=Ranboo points out, and Tubbo hums.
#Text=“Okay, then, how about..”
#Text=“No kisses all day if you don't get up.”
#Text=Ranboo's eyes widened, quickly letting go of Tubbo and springing out of the bed in a hurry.
#Text=“Alright!”
#Text=Ranboo suddenly outbursts, startling Tubbo in the act.
#Text=“Let's go do some chores today!
#Text=We have a lot to do and we shouldn't stay in bed any longer!”
#Text=Tubbo laughs so hard, jumping out of the bed as well.
#Text=“Is that really all it took?”
#Text=Ranboo sheepishly looks away, red-green blush covering their cheeks.
#Text=“I can't have my husband
#Text=not
#Text=give me kisses, that's like a fish without water!”
#Text=“So you're calling yourself a fish?”
#Text=Ranboo rolls his eyes, a falsetto of annoyance but Tubbo's been with them long enough to know when he's joking.
#Text=“That—I—
#Text=No
#Text=, I'm saying that I'll die without them.
#Text=The kisses, I mean.”
#Text=Tubbo scoffs, a faint blush hinting at his chubby cheeks and he pushes Ranboo out of the way of the master bedroom's door.
#Text=“You're dramatic, ridiculous, and extremely fucking embarrassing.”
#Text=Ranboo just smiles warmly, “I love you too.”
#Text=—
#Text=No matter how many times Tubbo was told by Captain Puffy or Foolish that taking care of a baby is a very hard responsibility, Tubbo actually disagrees.
#Text=To him, it's more of a way to realize how much he can see himself through a baby that can't even speak without hums and excessive crying.
#Text=Babies are—to his main understanding growing up—high maintenance and attention seeking.

#Text=At the time, which was around 14 to Tubbo, he didn't really understand why people would pay more attention to a baby than their other child, but it makes more sense now that Tubbo is older and now a father.
#Text=Because babies and children are going to be the center of your world, whether you allow it or not.
#Text=They will always be the first person you think of when making decisions, because anything you do should be for the better of them.
#Text=At least, that's what Tubbo feels like when he bottle-feeds Michael, that overbearing affection and warmth that seeps through his rough and scarred skin like an essential oil.
#Text=Maybe that's why he can see himself in Michael, sometimes.
#Text=Michael has a voice, and he uses it to communicate.
#Text=It doesn't necessarily have to be words for people to understand, but he still has a conscience to grab someone’s attention when he wants something or something to be done.
#Text=‘Kind of like me’
#Text=, Tubbo thinks.
#Text=After Michael had enough of the formula milk, Tubbo tips him over his shoulder and gently pats his back, waiting for that tiny burp.
#Text=A little noise, a hiccup, and Tubbo grins.
#Text=Michael yawns, snuggling into Tubbo’s shoulder and he coos.
#Text=“Oh wow, that was a
#Text=big
#Text=one huh, little man?”
#Text=Michael doesn't respond, not that Tubbo was expecting anything, he just sways in the dining seat, humming softly.
#Text=He doesn't know what he was humming, and Tubbo feels like he made it up on the spot.
#Text=Tubbo hears a small kiss noise on the top of Michael’s head, and knows exactly who did that.
#Text=“He reminds me of you, you know.”
#Text=Ranboo hums questioningly, sitting beside Tubbo at the dining table they used maybe 3 times in the year.
#Text=“He's a sleepy little baby, only sleeps and never wants to get up.
#Text=Like you.”
#Text=Tubbo grins at Ranboo's scoff.
#Text=“That—are you comparing me to a
#Text=three month old baby?
#Text=” He whispers.
#Text=Tubbo just cocks his head to the side, still swaying Michael on his shoulder gently.
#Text=They sit in silence for a little.
#Text=“You wanna hold him?”
#Text=Ranboo jumps at the sound of Tubbo’s voice, looking down to see Tubbo stare right back at him with his sapphire colored doe eyes.
#Text=“Um—I—I don't know if—” “it's okay, my love.
#Text=You don't have to if you don't want to.”
#Text=Ranboo melts.
#Text=He fiddles with their tie a little, promptly messing up the work Tubbo did.
#Text=From the little amount of time the two had Michael, he's only held him maybe (they think) once or twice.
#Text=Ranboo just gets really nervous holding something so,
#Text=so
#Text=small, with his big paws and abnormal height.
#Text=But, maybe this time..
#Text=“No, I—...I want to hold him.”
#Text=Tubbo hums, smiling so beautifully at Ranboo they think it's
#Text=very
#Text=unfair.
#Text=Tubbo slowly and softly picks up Michael, the baby only whimpering slightly before being given to Ranboo.
#Text=Ranboo nervously holds Michael like glass, Tubbo fixing up his position with the baby and then kisses their cheek.
#Text=Ranboo is completely frozen.
#Text=They don't want to move an inch so Michael has the best possible sleep, and he's also trying
#Text=so
#Text=hard not to look down and burst out a sob.
#Text=“Boo—hey,
#Text=relax,
#Text=babies can tell when you're tense.”
#Text=“Is that—is that a true fact?”
#Text=Tubbo shrugs, “I don't know, but you look like you're about to shit yourself.”
#Text=Ranboo cringes at the description of their expression, anxiously releases the tension in his shoulders and finally looks down at Michael.
#Text=Whatever words were threatening to spill out of Ranboo's mouth dissolved upon the unimaginable sight before him.
#Text=Michael was
#Text=so
#Text=tiny
#Text=in their arms.
#Text=Practically the size of a baby kitten, but it was so abnormal to Ranboo, his giant proportions making Michael completely dwarf in his hold.
#Text=The baby moves a little, turning his head more into Ranboo's forearm.
#Text=Tubbo immediately knows he's going to cry so he has the emergency handkerchief doused in a healing potion ready in his hand.
#Text=“
#Text=Oh god.”
#Text=“
#Text=Yeah.”
#Text=Ranboo looks at Michael and then back at Tubbo.
#Text=“How do you
#Text=not
#Text=start crying?
#Text=Like—All the time?”
#Text=Tubbo just rolls his eyes with no spite.
#Text=He dabs the creases of Ranboo's eyes with the handkerchief softy.
#Text=“Because I'm not a crybaby.”
#Text=Ranboo scoffs, grumbling something unintelligible under their breath.
#Text=Michael stirs, humming and snuggling more into Ranboo.
#Text=Ranboo melts even more than before, purring softly to themselves and Tubbo can't help but smile at the image presented.
#Text=“Looks like chores might have to wait, huh?”
#Text=Tubbo whispers, and Ranboo just nods sluggishly, purring like there was no tomorrow.
#Text=—
#Text=They didn't get anything done today.
#Text=The small family stayed inside, either cooing at Michael or staring longingly at each other like people disgustingly in love.
#Text=Tubbo
#Text=loved
#Text=it.
#Text=The two were getting ready for bed, Tubbo slipping into the comforters first.
#Text=He completely doused himself in the warmness, almost passing out immediately even after doing what he considered ‘nothing’ all day.
#Text=Soon after, Ranboo comes out of the bathroom, the room smelling like blazed watermelon and a hint of petunias.
#Text=“Wh—You already took like—
#Text=all
#Text=of the blankets, I see.”
#Text=Ranboo sarcastically comments, and Tubbo just hums low.
#Text=Ranboo chuckles, climbing into the bed and Tubbo pokes his head out of the comforters.
#Text=“Hey,” Ranboo whispers.
#Text=“Hi.”
#Text=“You come here often?”
#Text=“Well, it
#Text=is
#Text=my bed, so.”
#Text=Ranboo raises an eyebrow. “
#Text=Your
#Text=bed, huh?
#Text=What, so I'm—am I taking up your precious space on
#Text=your
#Text=bed?”
#Text=Tubbo unfolds the blanket off his face, looking directly at Ranboo with a serious face.
#Text=“Yes.”
#Text=“Oh, I'm hurt actually.
#Text=Just—Completely hurt.
#Text=Stabbed in the heart, even.”
#Text=“Mm well sucks to suck.”
#Text=Tubbo pulls the blanket over his face again, and Ranboo giggles.
#Text=Ranboo copies his movements, and the two turn to each other.
#Text=Just like in the morning, they get into their usual position and close their eyes, pretending to sleep.
#Text=“Can I have my kiss now,” Ranboo’s breath tickles Tubbo’s neck, and a familiar rose blush dusts the freckles of his skin.
#Text=“You're so spoiled.
#Text=But, I suppose.”
#Text=Petal lips meet bitten ones, but it only lasts so long.
#Text=Tubbo was always chaste with his kisses, but when Ranboo pulled back in, he knew the plan worked.
#Text=“Good night, my love.”
#Text=“Good night, sweetheart.”
#Text=And then the day repeats again.
